rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports and data types.
Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to define assumptions and guarantees; avoid implementation-specific subcomponent paths in type contracts.
generic_rules: Declare boolean range checks using explicit equality expressions with named limits.
Ensure AGREE types match AADL data types to prevent assumed/actual type mismatches.
Avoid encoding AADL properties like timing or unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` syntax.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference valid component features and ports.
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-level subcomponent ports in component type contracts.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean domain values into integers unless explicitly supported by the AADL model.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing-related AADL tokens
Implementation-level bindings and subcomponent port usage in type contracts
